items.title: Expanded 2026 North American World Cup: Spain's 'diligent' performance to Messi's enduring legacy
items.description: Eve Irvine is pleased to welcome Robbie Knock, Lead Commentator and sports journalist. The 2026 FIFA World Cup will be remembered not only for Spain's triumph on the pitch but also for the way football became enmeshed in politics, entertainment, institutional scrutiny, and the closing chapter of one of the sport's greatest careers. Robbie Knock argues that Spain's victory was the product of tactical superiority rather than fortune, while acknowledging that controversial refereeing decisions complicated perceptions of the tournament. He also presents Lionel Messi's expected international farewell as the conclusion of a defining era in world football, emphasising that dignity in defeat may ultimately reinforce rather than diminish his legacy.

items.title: Mary Honeyball praises forward-looking PM 'prepared to inject some optimism' into United Kingdom
items.description: François Picard is pleased to welcome Mary Honeyball, former Labour Member of the European Parliament from 2000 to 2019 representing London. She offers a cautiously optimistic assessment of the arrival of Andy Burnham to 10 Downing Street. She frames the moment less as a potential political reset after years of institutional fatigue. Mary Honeyball argues that the significance of the new premiership lies in the possibility of restoring public confidence, rebalancing the relationship between central and local government, and offering a more hopeful national narrative after the political and economic dislocation associated with Brexit.

items.description: Neuquén province will pitch foreign investors in New York on its first new international bond in nearly a decade as the local government reaps the rewards of a shale oil boom. Leer más

items.title: Canada-India defence collaboration for a secure future
items.description: Shared interests drive Canada and India toward a stronger strategic defence partnership

items.title: Russia says bus strike kills 5 in border region with Ukraine
items.description: The strike comes as Ukraine launched more than hundreds of drones at Moscow after a Russian missile barrage on Kyiv, as President Zelenskyy sought to calm growing tensions over Ukraine’s military leadership.

items.title: The days after
items.description: Parliamentary democracy is far from being a synonym for productivity. Yet all that and more lies after the challenge of tomorrow’s World Cup final. Leer más

items.title: Spain won the World Cup, but Donald Trump 'cast a shadow'
items.description: Though Donald Trump mostly stayed clear of stadiums, the World Cup may influence the US midterm elections. DW looks at the political implications of "the most successful sporting event maybe in the history of the world."

items.title: Messi on eve of final: Argentina has already written history 'no-one can erase'
items.description: Argentina captain says the team's greatest achievement has been the journey rather than the trophies as he delivers an emotional message to fans before Sunday's World Cup final. Leer más

items.title: Philippines accuses Chinese coast guard of injuring Filipino sailor in disputed shoal
items.description: Chinese and Philippines authorities are blaming each other for a row on Monday between Chinese coast guard personnel and Philippine sailors in a disputed shoal in the South China Sea

items.title: Google plans new chip to run Gemini models more efficiently, the Information reports
At least 930 people have died in Congo's Ebola outbreak
items.description: Thirty-seven new deaths were recorded in the previous 24 hours in one of the highest single-day totals

items.title: Ghana court jails opposition party's regional chairman for illegal mining
items.description: A Ghanaian High Court has sentenced Bernard Antwi Boasiako, popularly known as Chairman Wontumi, to 20 years in prison with hard labour for illegal mining.

items.title: Hiding behind the World Cup, Milei siblings restructure their government
items.description: The World Cup provided the Milei administration a much-needed breather after three months under siege. Javier and Karina Milei are learning by doing, but by doing so they are slowly but surely resembling what they initially promised to battle. Leer más

items.title: Stories that caught our eye: July 10 to 17
items.description: A selection of stories that caught our eye over the last seven days in Argentina. Leer más

items.title: ‘Our bodies contain poison’: Landmark trial in Argentina probes dangers of agrochemicals
items.description: Trial over agrochemical spraying in the city of Pergamino has set an unprecedented legal benchmark: for the first time, a federal court placed the entire agricultural production chain in the dock, from farmers and agronomists to public officials. Trial ends in acquittal for nearly all those excused, except two municipal employees. Leer más

items.title: Duo sails toilet to Kangaroo Island on homemade barge
items.description: When two mates "sailed" a toilet barge across Backstairs Passage to Kangaroo Island, there was more to it than just having a laugh.

items.title: Andy Burnham becomes U.K. PM, promising new social and political model
items.description: ‘I go with good grace and a smile on my face’, says outgoing Prime Minister Keir Starmer says

items.title: India's Gen Z 'Cockroach People's Party' started as satire but is serious about change
items.description: They call themselves the "cockroaches" and they're on the march — demanding action against corruption, amid a shortage of opportunities for young people in India.

items.title: Argentina players receive heroes’ welcome home despite World Cup defeat
items.description: Argentina’s players were welcomed home by fans lining the streets as the team travelled from the airport.

items.title: Milei says won't attend World Cup final out of superstition
items.description: British calls on FIFA to investigate after Argentina's players held up a banner reading "Las Malvinas son Argentinas" after their victory over England. Leer más

items.title: 3 U.S. personnel killed in Iran conflict. And, Hunter Biden opens up to NPR
items.description: Three U.S. service members have been killed since Friday amid rising tension between the U.S. and Iran. And, Hunter Biden talks to NPR's Newsmakers about addiction and public scrutiny.

items.title: President Murmu arrives in Moldova; first by any Indian head of State
items.description: As part of the first leg of her visit, President Droupadi Murmu will hold delegation-level talks with her Moldovan counterpart, President Maia Sandu

items.title: Ukraine fires 400 missiles at Moscow after deadly Russian attacks
items.description: Ukraine fired more than 400 drones toward Moscow in its latest major attack on the Russian capital on July 20. The blitz came hours after Russia bombarded Kyiv and other Ukrainian cities with ballistic missiles in a relentless cycle of tit-for-tat strikes.

items.title: Randa Abdel-Fattah appears to abandon threatened defamation case against SA Premier
items.description: Earlier this year, Palestinian-Australian author Randa Abdel-Fattah said she had sent two concerns notices to SA Premier Peter Malinauskas over comments made around the time of her controversial removal from the line-up of this year's Adelaide Writers' Week.

items.title: 'Her life is ruined': Hellenic Club cleared of failing to record problem gambling
items.description: The Hellenic Club has been cleared of breaches of failing to record the problem gambling of a Canberra couple despite the pair losing at least $345,000 on poker machines.

items.title: Boy, 13, dies during World Cup celebrations in Spain
items.description: Two other young people were injured after a fountain partially collapsed in Salamanca's Ciudad Rodrigo.

items.title: Fears over Gulf shipping and water rise as US-Iran trade strikes
items.description: The United States and Iran have continued to exchange fire on July 20th, raising fears for Gulf shipping and water supplies in the region. After Kuwait reported Iranian attacks on water and electricity plants as well as oil facilities over the weekend, Bahrain said Iran this Monday, July 20th attacked civil air navigation systems. Despite this, Tehran says communication with Washington remains active through diplomatic channels.

items.title: UK's new PM Andy Burnham cuts household electricity tax
items.description: The UK's new leader moved to remove VAT from electricity bills from October 1. The move comes before Burnham chairs his first cabinet meeting later today.

items.title: ‘We just want to live’: Palestinians in Gaza react to Hamas election
items.description: There are mixed reactions in Gaza after Khalil al-Hayya is elected Hamas leader.

items.title: From Maradona to Messi: Bangladesh's enduring love for Argentina
items.description: The craze for Argentina has endured for decades in Bangladesh, a country of 170 million people who have never qualified for the World Cup. Leer más

items.title: Gaborone accuses Moscow of luring Batswana to battlefield
items.description: Botswana is the latest nation to accuse Moscow of using deceptive tactics to lure foreign recruits to the battlefield. Without saying how many of its citizens were involved, Gaborone's foreign ministry claimed its nationals were duped into going to Russia and then forced into active combat.

items.title: Israelis march to Gaza border demanding re-establishment of settlements
Rubio says United States wants to globalise fight against far-left 'terrorism'
items.description: Argentina among nations represented at meeting on "The Resurgence of Political Terrorism" hosted by US Secretary of State Marco Rubio. Leer más

items.title: Paramount and Warner Bros mega merger paused by judge
items.description: US court temporarily stops Paramount and Warner Bros merging after 12 states launched legal action.

items.title: Hobart prison sourcing meals from Victoria after floor fault shuts $19m kitchen
items.description: A newly built commercial kitchen at Hobart's Risdon Prison has shut just three months after officially opening due to construction faults, leaving staff to source daily meals for about 840 inmates from Victoria.

items.title: An expanding regional war
Why protests are growing in Ukraine over defence leadership and dismissals
items.description: Protests continue as anger grows over President Zelenskyy’s decision to fire the country’s defence minister.

items.title: US ramps up its global battle against abortion as women in Africa face health risks
items.description: For decades, U.S. anti-abortion groups have lobbied domestically and abroad for restricting access to abortion.

items.title: Nigeria and Ghana call on AU to address 'Afrophobic protests' in South Africa
items.description: Nigeria and Ghana have condemned what they called the recent "Afrophobic protests targeting African nationals in South Africa." In a statement, Nigeria's ministry of foreign affairs said the two countries want the issue placed on the agenda at the next African Union summit.

items.title: U.S., Iran trade fire for seventh consecutive day
items.description: The United States and Iran exchanged strikes aimed at infrastructure and military targets on Saturday as an Iranian negotiator said Tehran had suspended its commitments under the interim deal with the U.S. — snapping another fragile thread as the war shows no end in sight

items.title: Milei almost halved education and science spending, reports finds
items.description: University of Buenos Aires researchers say education investment has almost halved in two years since President Javier Milei took office, while second Grupo EPC analysis says funding for science has fallen by more than 46%. Leer más

items.title: Cities are getting smarter about water and other eco wins
items.description: From flood-resistant infrastructure to new ways of securing drinking water, cities are adapting to the growing climate pressures of a hotter, drier world. Plus, tourists pitching in to help sea turtles.

items.title: Argentina's press laments Albiceleste defeat, hails end of an era
items.description: Press reflects on Messi's emotional farewell and an uncertain future; Headlines focus on tears, farewell fears and the end of an era. Leer más

items.title: Andy Burnham: The King in the North
Five teens plead guilty over deadly train station ambush
items.description: Four boys and one girl have pleaded guilty to manslaughter, after a man with an acquired brain injury was lured to a train station on the NSW South Coast.

items.title: Messi eyes glorious farewell as Spain, Argentina clash in World Cup final
items.description: Holders Argentina are bidding to become the first team in 64 years to successfully defend the title, in what likely to be the final World Cup match of captain Lionel Messi's career. Leer más

items.title: AliExpress hit with record EU fine
items.description: The Chinese retailer has become the latest major online marketplace to face penalties under the EU’s Digital Services Act.

items.title: U.K. PM Burnham names Reynolds as Business Minister, Rayner for housing
items.description: Jonathan Reynolds was named ‌Secretary ⁠of State for ⁠Business, Innovation, Science and Trade in an expanded role ​after losing his ‌position as business Minister to Peter Kyle in a reshuffle under ‌former Prime Minister ​Keir Starmer

items.title: US attacks Iran for 10th consecutive night
items.description: Washington vows revenge for US military casualties, while Iran claims attacks on US sites across the region.
